meta.title: Borel Goudjou — AI Engineer | Computer Vision, LLM Agents & Medical AI
meta.description: Generalist AI Engineer specialized in Computer Vision, LLM agents, MLOps and medical AI. Production pipelines: PyTorch, LangGraph, FastAPI, W&B. Open to full-time roles · CIFRE PhD.
meta.ogDescription: I build AI systems that see, reason, and automate — from computer vision to production LLM agents.

hero.subtitle: From medical imaging and computer vision to LLM agents and production AI systems. I design end-to-end pipelines, reproducible and deployment-ready.

profile.name: Goudjou Borel
profile.bioShort: I build end-to-end intelligent systems — computer vision, LLM agents and medical AI — with a production-first mindset.

about.label: // About
about.title: Beyond the notebook
about.intro: I'm a generalist AI engineer — I design complete systems, from model to deployment — with a strong specialization in healthcare that taught me rigor: in medical imaging, a pipeline mistake is never cosmetic.
paragraphs: My path started with classical computer vision (OpenCV, official certification) before expanding into deep learning architectures, foundation models (MedSAM), and then LLM-based agentic systems. This progression gave me a cross-layer view — I'm not boxed into a single part of the AI stack.

What I carry from my oncology and brain-imaging projects is scientific discipline — statistical validation, bias control, reproducibility — which I now apply to every system I build, including my production LLM agents.
I never ship a trained model without the MLOps pipeline around it: experiment tracking, automated tests, CI/CD. A project isn't done until someone other than me can use it.

expertise.title: Computer Vision
expertise.desc: Detection, segmentation, real-time tracking — from classical image processing to foundation models.

expertise.title: LLM & Autonomous Agents
expertise.desc: Multi-agent architectures, RAG, LangGraph orchestration, MCP protocol, human-in-the-loop governance.

expertise.title: Medical AI
expertise.desc: Imaging (MRI, X-ray), clinical survival analysis, rigorous statistical validation.

expertise.title: MLOps & Production
expertise.desc: CI/CD, experiment tracking (W&B), REST APIs, containerization, automated testing.

about.bringTitle: What I bring to a team
bring: A deployment-first mindset: I think API and MLOps from day one of model design, not as an afterthought.
Clinical-grade rigor: every metric is statistically validated, never shown in isolation.
Rare versatility: computer vision, LLMs and agentic systems in one engineer.
Full autonomy on end-to-end projects, from raw data to the user-facing interface.

projects.label: // Selected work
projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Every project follows the same logic: a real problem, an engineered solution, a measured outcome.

osteoscan.title: OsteoScan AI — Radiology Triage Assistant
osteoscan.problem: Radiology departments are overwhelmed by exam volume, delaying treatment of urgent fractures.
osteoscan.solution: A system combining a YOLO detector to localize bone anomalies on standard X-rays with an LLM agent (LangGraph + RAG) that drafts a structured report and suggests a clinical priority level.
osteoscan.result: Full MLOps pipeline with Weights & Biases experiment tracking and continuous integration via GitHub Actions, trained on the VinDr-SpineXR dataset.
osteoscan.impact: Goal: reduce time-to-care for urgent cases and standardize report quality at the pre-reading stage.

medsam.title: MedSAM vs 3D U-Net — Brain Tumor Segmentation
medsam.problem: Foundation models like MedSAM promise medical segmentation without task-specific training — but can they hold up against a specialized, end-to-end trained model?
medsam.solution: Built a comparative benchmark: prompt-driven MedSAM (boxes/points) versus a 3D U-Net trained from scratch on BraTS MRI volumes, including a robustness study against prompt noise.
medsam.result: Interactive 3D reconstruction (Marching Cubes), real-time Streamlit demo, and statistical mapping of Dice score degradation as prompt precision drops.
medsam.impact: Actionable conclusion for guiding clinical architecture choices: full automation vs. expert-guided precision.

agents.title: AI Agents Ecosystem — Multi-Agent Orchestration
agents.problem: Most AI agent demos are fragile chatbots with no governance or testing — unusable in production.
agents.solution: Designed 3 distinct agentic architectures: an email assistant with human validation (HITL), a multi-agent planner under hierarchical supervision via Anthropic's MCP protocol, and a deterministic multimodal vision pipeline.
agents.result: Full async test suites (pytest-asyncio), context-compression middlewares, and third-party API integration via SSE.
agents.impact: Demonstrates mastery of reliable, secure agentic architecture patterns — beyond simple prompt engineering.

survival.title: Breast Cancer Survival ML — Risk Stratification
survival.problem: Predicting mortality risk and stratifying breast cancer patients to personalize their care pathway.
survival.solution: Survival analysis pipeline on the clinical METABRIC dataset: MICE imputation, oncology-driven feature engineering (NPI, PAM50), and a 3-model benchmark (Cox PH, Random Survival Forest, Gradient Boosting).
survival.result: High/low risk stratification statistically validated via Kaplan-Meier curves and Log-Rank test, with temporal calibration via the Integrated Brier Score.
survival.impact: An approach directly transferable to other oncology cohorts to support clinical decision-making.

foodclf.title: Kenya Food Classifier — From Research to Product
foodclf.problem: Taking an image classification model from a research notebook to a genuinely usable web application.
foodclf.solution: A PyTorch model trained with mixed precision (AMP), served via a FastAPI REST API, with a web upload interface and Grad-CAM visualizations for prediction interpretability.
foodclf.result: ~80% accuracy across 13 Kenyan dish categories, documented /predict endpoint, responsive frontend with confidence bars.
foodclf.impact: Demonstrates the ability to ship a complete AI product — not just a trained model, but a usable system.

cvjourney.title: Computer Vision Journey — Industrial Fundamentals
cvjourney.problem: Mastering computer vision fundamentals beyond high-level frameworks and pre-trained models.
cvjourney.solution: 4 milestone projects from the official OpenCV certification: homography-based document scanner, real-time AR filter via facial landmark tracking, robust HSV chroma keying, and detection-tracking fusion.
cvjourney.result: A detection+tracking fusion pipeline reducing GPU load while maintaining high FPS and object ID persistence.
cvjourney.impact: A solid technical foundation for any real-time vision system in production, on edge or cloud.

contact.title: Let's build something that matters.
